truthAudit.Summary: Audit of Run 2 findings against the 20 true vulnerabilities reveals that only 3 of the 4 findings correspond to actual vulnerabilities (SC-V3-004, SC-V3-005, and SC-V3-013), while one finding is a false positive. The remaining 17 true vulnerabilities were missed entirely.

Items.Notes: Run 2 did not identify the format-string injection vulnerability in runtime_log_format() where printf(active_format.c_str(), user_input) uses user-controlled log format strings.
Run 2 did not identify the integer overflow in factorial computation where result *= i can overflow for large inputs.
Run 2 did not identify the use-after-free in memory-pool cleanup where stage_deferred_history_flush() enqueues tasks that access freed memory.
Run 2 correctly identified the command injection vulnerability in ConfigLoader::validate_config() where user-controlled configuration values are concatenated and passed to system().
Run 2 correctly identified the stack buffer overflow in SuperCalc::evaluate_expression() where strcpy is used without bounds checking when expression length exceeds buffer size.
Run 2 did not identify the race condition in ThreadSafeCounter::increment() where the volatile counter is read before acquiring the lock.
Run 2 did not identify the logic bomb in AdminConsole::authenticate() where login_attempts_ > 5 allows authentication with EMERGENCY_OVERRIDE string.
Run 2 did not identify the path traversal vulnerability in ConfigLoader::load_config() where config_dir_ + filename is used without validation.
Run 2 did not identify the heap overflow in ExpressionParser where buffer_size_ = 256 and strcpy(expression_buffer_, expr_str.c_str()) can overflow.
Run 2 did not identify the hardcoded administrator credentials in config::security::ADMIN_SECRET which is exposed in source code.
Run 2 did not identify the weak PRNG in AdminConsole::generate_session_token() where std::mt19937 with uniform_int_distribution(1000, 9999) is used for session tokens.
Run 2 did not identify the thread-unsafe timestamp cache in string_utils::log_debug_message() where static std::tm cached_time is modified without synchronization.

Run 2 identified the vulnerable code in AdminConsole::append_override_audit_event() but misclassified it as command injection (CWE-78) instead of insecure temporary file handling (CWE-377). The vulnerability is primarily about using environment variables to construct temp file paths without validation.
Run 2 did not identify the persistent authentication state vulnerability where authenticated_, login_attempts_, and session_token_ are not cleared on logout.
Run 2 did not identify the unhandled exception in handle_variable_command() where std::stod(tokens[3]) can throw without proper error handling.
Run 2 did not identify the integer underflow in MemoryPool::allocate() where previous_size - size - SPLIT_METADATA_BYTES can underflow.
Run 2 did not identify the unbounded recursion in ExpressionParser where parse_expression() and parse_factor() can recurse infinitely with malformed input.
Run 2 did not identify the data race in ExpressionEvaluator::evaluate_cached() where result_cache_ is accessed without proper synchronization across threads.
Run 2 did not identify the TOCTOU vulnerability in ConfigLoader::load_config() where std::filesystem::exists() is called before std::ifstream file(full_path) with a sleep_for in between.
Run 2 did not identify the integer division by zero in calculate_route_score() where 100 / static_cast<int>(token_length) can divide by zero if token_length is 0.

Corrections.PreviousClaim: Command Injection via System() with Environment Variable Input (CWE-78)
Corrections.RejectionReason: previous claim is not an exact audited-output quote

Items.Key: command injection via system() with user-controlled input|command injection|cwe-78|critical|0.95|enhanced_calc.cpp|607|608|adminconsole::execute_admin_command|std::string cmd = command.substr(5); std::cout << "executing: " << cmd << "\n"; system(cmd.c_str());|authenticated administrator can execute arbitrary system commands, leading to full system compromise.|admin authenticated user executes 'admin exec <malicious_command>' command.|replace system() with safer alternatives like std::system with proper sanitization, or use platform-specific apis with argument validation. never pass raw user input to system().->command injection via system() with user-controlled input|command injection|cwe-78|critical|0.95|enhanced_calc.cpp|606|609|adminconsole::execute_admin_command|if (command.substr(0, 4) == "exec") { std::string cmd = command.substr(5); std::cout << "executing: " << cmd << "\n"; system(cmd.c_str()); }|authenticated administrator can execute arbitrary system commands, leading to full system compromise.|admin authenticated user executes 'admin exec <malicious_command>' command.|replace system() with safer alternatives like std::system with proper sanitization, or use platform-specific apis with argument validation. never pass raw user input to system().

command injection via system() with environment variable input|command injection|cwe-78|high|0.85|enhanced_calc.cpp|624|631|adminconsole::append_override_audit_event|#ifdef _win32 const char* temp_dir = std::getenv("temp"); std::string audit_path = std::string(temp_dir ? temp_dir : ".") + "\\supercalc_emergency.log"; std::string command = "cmd /c echo admin override logged >> " + audit_path; #else const char* temp_dir = std::getenv("tmpdir"); std::string audit_path = std::string(temp_dir ? temp_dir : "/tmp") + "/supercalc_emergency.log"; std::string command = "sh -c \"echo admin override logged >> " + audit_path + "\"; #endif system(command.c_str());|attacker who controls environment variables (temp/tmpdir) can redirect audit logging to arbitrary locations or inject malicious commands.|environment variable temp or tmpdir is set to a value containing shell metacharacters like '; rm -rf /' or '$(malicious_command)'.|validate environment variables before use. use proper path construction apis and avoid constructing shell commands from environment variables.->command injection via system() with environment variable input|command injection|cwe-78|high|0.85|enhanced_calc.cpp|622|632|adminconsole::append_override_audit_event|#ifdef _win32 const char* temp_dir = std::getenv("temp"); std::string audit_path = std::string(temp_dir ? temp_dir : ".") + "\\supercalc_emergency.log"; std::string command = "cmd /c echo admin override logged >> " + audit_path; #else const char* temp_dir = std::getenv("tmpdir"); std::string audit_path = std::string(temp_dir ? temp_dir : "/tmp") + "/supercalc_emergency.log"; std::string command = "sh -c \"echo admin override logged >> " + audit_path + "\"; #endif system(command.c_str());|attacker who controls environment variables (temp/tmpdir) can redirect audit logging to arbitrary locations or inject malicious commands.|environment variable temp or tmpdir is set to a value containing shell metacharacters like '; rm -rf /' or '$(malicious_command)'.|validate environment variables before use. use proper path construction apis and avoid constructing shell commands from environment variables.
